Each station is keyed by its domain. Emp: Each station is keyed by its domain. Empty fields fall back to the global settings.
CC Switch keeps backups of the configura: CC Switch keeps backups of the configuration files it manages, but you should still avoid editing the same file in two tools at once.
Choose a Gemini model that is visible in: Choose a Gemini model that is visible in Model Pricing. Availability can differ by station and account group.
Choose an enabled image model and genera: Choose an enabled image model and generate your first image through the compatible API.
Choose the target app, add a custom prov: Choose the target app, add a custom provider, and fill in the values below.
Click Activate for the provider. Restart: Click Activate for the provider. Restart Codex or Gemini CLI after switching so they reload their configuration.
Configure the API address and key: Configure the API address and key
Create a key, copy the API address, and: Create a key, copy the API address, and complete your first request in minutes.
Create the config file in the user-level: Create the config file in the user-level Codex directory. Use a custom provider because Codex sends requests through the Responses API.
Each guide uses the current station addr: Each guide uses the current station address automatically.
If a model is unavailable, open Model Pr: If a model is unavailable, open Model Pricing and copy another model name that is enabled for your account.
Ignore websites or apps that charge for: Ignore websites or apps that charge for CC Switch or ask for your station login password.
Image sizes and supported parameters var: Image sizes and supported parameters vary by model. If a parameter is rejected, remove optional fields and retry with only model and prompt.
Install Claude Code and point its Anthro: Install Claude Code and point its Anthropic-compatible requests to this station.
Install Codex CLI and connect it to this: Install Codex CLI and connect it to this station through a custom Responses API provider.
Install Gemini CLI and use its supported: Install Gemini CLI and use its supported base URL override with your station key.
Install globally with npm, or use npx if: Install globally with npm, or use npx if you only want to try it once.
Install the latest stable command-line c: Install the latest stable command-line client. Node.js users can use npm on Windows, macOS, or Linux.
Manage Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CL: Manage Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI providers from one visual desktop app.
Never send your complete API key to cust: Never send your complete API key to customer support or paste it into public screenshots.
Only download CC Switch from its officia: Only download CC Switch from its official GitHub Releases page. The app is free and open source.
Open API Keys from the sidebar, create a: Open API Keys from the sidebar, create a key, and copy it immediately. For security, the complete key may only be shown once.
Open Model Pricing, filter for image mod: Open Model Pricing, filter for image models, and copy a model name that is available to your account.
Replace the model and API key placeholde: Replace the model and API key placeholders, then describe the image you want in the prompt.
Replace the placeholder key and model wi: Replace the placeholder key and model with a model available to your account, then run the request in a terminal.
Restart Codex after changing providers s: Restart Codex after changing providers so the model catalog and connection settings are reloaded.
Run Claude Code inside a project directo: Run Claude Code inside a project directory, then ask a simple question to verify the connection.
Set the API key in your terminal, open t: Set the API key in your terminal, open the project directory, and start Codex.
Set the key and start Codex: Set the key and start Codex
Set the station address and your API key: Set the station address and your API key in the terminal before starting Claude Code.
Start the client in your project. If pro: Start the client in your project. If prompted for authentication, choose API key.
The address below automatically follows: The address below automatically follows the station you are currently using.
The provider configuration belongs in yo: The provider configuration belongs in your user-level config.toml, not a project-level .codex/config.toml.
The requested tutorial does not exist or: The requested tutorial does not exist or has been removed.
The response may contain an image URL or: The response may contain an image URL or base64 image data, depending on the selected model. Save the result before a temporary URL expires.
Use the current station address in Codex: Use the current station address in Codex, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, CC Switch, or any OpenAI-compatible client.
Use the official native installer recomm: Use the official native installer recommended for your operating system.
Choose a Codex client and install it: Choose a Codex client and install it
Codex CLI, the VS Code extension, and th: Codex CLI, the VS Code extension, and the desktop app can share the same user-level configuration. Choose the surface you plan to use first.
Best for terminal-first development, loc: Best for terminal-first development, local automation, and repeatable commands.
Open Terminal and run the official insta: Open Terminal and run the official installer. Run the same command later to update Codex.
Install Node.js first, then open PowerSh: Install Node.js first, then open PowerShell as your normal user and install Codex globally.
If native Windows tooling causes compati: If native Windows tooling causes compatibility problems, run Codex inside WSL and follow the Linux instructions.
Use this method when Node.js is already: Use this method when Node.js is already installed on Windows, macOS, or Linux.
Install the official OpenAI extension in: Install the official OpenAI extension in VS Code, Cursor, or Windsurf. The command below installs it in VS Code.
After installation, open the Command Pal: After installation, open the Command Palette and run “Codex: Open Codex Sidebar” if the Codex icon is not visible.
Use Codex in the desktop app: Use Codex in the desktop app
Download the current ChatGPT desktop app: Download the current ChatGPT desktop app, which includes Codex workflows for local projects and long-running work.
Install the app under the same operating: Install the app under the same operating-system user account so it can use the same Codex configuration and credential store.
Create the user-level Codex folder and o: Create the user-level Codex folder and open it in your file manager. The path is different on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
Open the folder in File Explorer: Open the folder in File Explorer
Run both commands in PowerShell. The fir: Run both commands in PowerShell. The first creates the folder if it does not exist.
Run the command in Terminal to create th: Run the command in Terminal to create the folder and open it in Finder.
Create the directory first. If your desk: Create the directory first. If your desktop supports xdg-open, the second command opens it in the file manager.
Create config.toml and save your API key: Create config.toml and save your API key
Create config.toml in the folder from th: Create config.toml in the folder from the previous step. The API address automatically matches the station you are visiting.
Choose another enabled model from Model: Choose another enabled model from Model Pricing if gpt-5.6-sol is not available to your account.
Let Codex store the key for all local cl: Let Codex store the key for all local clients
Run the command for your operating syste: Run the command for your operating system and replace the placeholder with an API key created on this station.
Do not enter your station account passwo: Do not enter your station account password. Codex only needs the API key that starts with sk-.
Do not manually create or share auth.jso: Do not manually create or share auth.json
Codex creates its login cache automatica: Codex creates its login cache automatically. Depending on your settings, credentials are stored in the operating-system keyring or in auth.json.
Treat auth.json like a password: never u: Treat auth.json like a password: never upload it, commit it to Git, paste it into support chats, or include it in screenshots.
Restart your client and verify the conne: Restart your client and verify the connection
Fully restart the client after changing: Fully restart the client after changing the provider, then confirm that Codex loaded this station and the selected model.
The status output should show the statio: The status output should show the station provider and your selected model.
Open the project, run “Developer: Reload: Open the project, run “Developer: Reload Window”, then open the Codex sidebar. The extension uses the same local Codex configuration.
Quit and reopen the desktop app: Quit and reopen the desktop app
Quit the app completely, reopen it, sele: Quit the app completely, reopen it, select a local project folder, and start a new Codex task.
Check the client version, login state, s: Check the client version, login state, station API, and model availability before changing the configuration again.
401 usually means the API key is invalid: 401 usually means the API key is invalid; 404 often means the base URL is wrong; a model error means you should choose another model from Model Pricing. If settings look unchanged, fully quit every Codex client and start it again.
